title: Mibora minima
intro: Mibora minima, also known as the little powan or dwarf powan, is a small, annual grass-like plant belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing due to its high content of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The bioactive constituents of Mibora minima include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other phenolic compounds, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Mibora minima is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
